topic: Escape.tech AI API Security Review 2026: Business Logic and Complex Auth Testing

key_points: Independent review of Escape as an API DAST tool
Covers ease of setup, scan accuracy, and CI/CD integration
Notes strong business logic detection compared to legacy tools
Escape focuses on pre-production scanning vs Salt's runtime defense
Salt lacks shift-left capabilities and native pre-production testing
Traffic mirroring in Salt is expensive (~$0.015 per ENI/hour)
Noname (Akamai) requires capturing traffic from all production environments
Setup can take months to years in large organizations
Escape provides faster time-to-value with schema-based testing
Lists top 5 API security solutions for 2026
Positions Escape alongside StackHawk, Salt, APIsec, and Akamai
Differentiates tools by pre-production vs runtime coverage
User reviews highlight fast onboarding and accurate findings
Cons include steep pricing for high API volumes and occasional false positives

stats.fact: Escape delivers 3,900% coverage improvement over legacy DAST solutions for business logic testing
AI and API vulnerabilities soared nearly 400% year over year, rising from 439 in 2024 to 2,185 in 2025
67% of API vulnerabilities are rated High or Critical impact, yet 56% are exploitable by low-skill actors
68% of organizations admit they don't have complete visibility into their API attack surface (2026 State of AppSec survey)
APIs accounted for 17% of all reported security vulnerabilities (11,053 of 67,058 published bulletins)
Escape raised $18M Series A in March 2026 to scale AI agent-driven discovery, pentesting, and remediation
Escape is trusted by 2,000+ security teams and GraphQL Armor middleware has 100,000+ weekly npm downloads
Development velocity increased 3x for teams using AI coding assistants, but API security testing coverage only grew 1.4x

angles: Deep-dive review of Escape.tech as the only DAST with a feedback-driven Business Logic Security Testing engine
How Escape handles complex authentication flows (OAuth 2.0, JWT, MFA, browser-based) that break traditional scanners

Real developer workflow: integrating Escape into CI/CD pipelines for continuous API security
Post-$18M Series A: what Escape's AI pentesting agent roadmap means for AppSec teams in 2026
